title: malva alcea subsp. tournefortiana
intro: Malva alcea subsp. tournefortiana, also known as Great Mallow or Alcea, is a perennial herb belonging to the Malvaceae family. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The health benefits of Malva alcea subsp. tournefortiana include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams to utilize its medicinal properties and alleviate various health conditions.
